commentary_text: The model assigns Japan a 69% win probability while the market settles at 45%, a 24-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in Japan. The market also prices Sweden's chances at 28% versus the model's 7%, a 21-point spread in the opposite direction. Both sources agree Japan is the favorite, but the market substantially hedges that view. This divergence may reflect information unavailable to the model's last update—recent lineup changes, injury developments, or shifts in team form since the model was calibrated—that have shifted real-money assessments toward a more competitive match. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal worth monitoring; it does not resolve whether the model's structural advantage or the market's real-time information captures the true probabilities.
